subject: Outlook push notifications not arriving on iPhone
description: User does not hear or see alerts for new Outlook emails; opening the app manually shows unread messages that arrived minutes ago.

ts_steps.title: Check notification settings
ts_steps.action: Settings → Notifications → Outlook — ensure Allow Notifications is on; set Alert Style to Banners or Alerts; enable Sounds and Badges.

ts_steps.title: Check Focus mode
ts_steps.action: Settings → Focus — if a Focus mode (e.g. Do Not Disturb, Work) is active, add Outlook to the allowed apps list or disable the Focus temporarily to test.

ts_steps.title: Reinstall Outlook
ts_steps.action: Delete Outlook app, restart iPhone, reinstall from App Store, sign in; this resets the push notification token with Apple's push service.

internal_information: Outlook for iOS uses its own push infrastructure via Microsoft servers, not native Exchange push. A stale APNs token after an iOS update is a common cause. Low Power Mode can also defer notifications.
